steps.title: Spot the noun phrase structure
steps.content: On the left of the blank: 'geologist,' a noun that is part of the noun phrase 'American geologist.' On the right: 'Marie Tharp,' a proper noun followed by a comma and an appositive phrase.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
No punctuation should separate noun modifiers from the proper names they describe, so the sentence should leave 'geologist' and 'Marie Tharp' together, with the comma appearing after the complete unit to introduce the appositive.

The other choices all insert punctuation that breaks this rule.

That matches B.
